prd_refs: §9.7 — project config schema and discovery semantics.
§9.13 — TDD: project config discovery + disabling via env.
description: Walk upward from the current directory to find `./.slashops.json`. Stop at the filesystem root or when found. Respect `SLASHOPS_NO_PROJECT_CONFIG=1` (skip discovery entirely). Project paths in the config (e.g. safe write roots) resolve relative to the project config file location.
notes: Document that project config does not get loaded when called from a global script with no `$PWD` context (caller must supply explicit start dir).
steps: TDD: Discovery returns `$null` when no `.slashops.json` exists in any ancestor.
TDD: Discovery returns the closest ancestor's path when one exists.
TDD: `SLASHOPS_PROJECT_CONFIG` env var, when set, takes precedence over walk-up.
TDD: Walk-up stops at the filesystem root without infinite loop.
Author `Private/Config/Resolve-ProjectConfig.ps1` taking `[string]$StartDirectory = $PWD` and returning the resolved path or `$null`.
Pester tests use `TestDrive:` to create nested directory trees with and without `.slashops.json`.
name: [cfg-06] Project config discovery (./.slashops.json walk-up)
